page: Microsoft Azure E4bs_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the EBSv5 family. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. This configuration consists of 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The processor includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage or onboard GPU included with this instance, but it is Premium Storage capable and optimized for block storage performance.

In benchmark evaluations, the E4bs_v5 demonstrates moderate single-core performance alongside weaker multi-core scaling. The stress-ng single-core CPU test scored 1894.78 (average), while the multi-core CPU test reached 3874.86 (weak). Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores are 1639.0 (average) and 3539.0 (weak), respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show 208.44 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 29.36 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads, both falling into the weak category, though large-block write bandwidth is average at 25.78 GB/sec. Single-threaded operations like gzip compression (28.53 GB/sec) and bzip3 decompression (10.29 GB/sec) perform at average levels, whereas multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is poor at 19.61 GB/sec.

Given its 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the E4bs_v5 offers a memory-optimized profile suitable for workloads requiring a larger memory footprint relative to compute power. It is designed for applications that leverage external block storage, as it lacks local disks but supports high-performance Premium Storage. The instance is suited for relational databases, caching layers like Redis (which achieved 3.81 million SET operations per second in testing), and memory-intensive enterprise applications. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and high memory density provide a stable environment for memory-bound tasks, though compute-heavy workloads may encounter performance bottlenecks due to the lower multi-core benchmark results.
description: Microsoft Azure E4bs_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the EBSv5 family, featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, it provides 2 physical cores with hyper-threading and a 48 MB L3 cache. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but is Premium Storage capable, focusing on high block storage performance. Benchmark results indicate average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1639.0, but weaker multi-core scaling, as shown by a multi-core score of 3539.0. Memory write bandwidth is moderate at 25.78 GB/sec, while read bandwidth remains lower. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that do not require heavy parallel compute, making it suitable for database operations, caching systems, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E4bs_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and premium block storage support, designed for memory-bound enterprise workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and premium block storage support for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
No local storage, optimized instead for external Premium Storage
Average single-core performance with weaker multi-core scaling in benchmarks
Best suited for database operations, caching layers, and memory-bound applications
